message:
fix: add dolt-access.lock and noms LOCK detection and cleanup to bd doctor

Add diagnostic detection and `bd doctor --fix` cleanup for two Dolt lock
types that cause "database is locked by another dolt process" errors after
crashes:

- dolt-access.lock: embedded Dolt advisory flock (age-based, 5 min threshold)
- .beads/dolt/<db>/.dolt/noms/LOCK: Dolt internal filesystem lock

The noms LOCK cleanup uses flock probing to verify no active process holds
the advisory lock before removing — more correct than age-only cleanup.

Also updates database.go recovery steps to reference Dolt lock files instead
of legacy SQLite artifacts.

Addresses #1661.

Co-authored-by: seanbearden <seanbearden@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sjsyrek <sjsyrek@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/crew/emma
Rig: beads
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -522,8 +522,8 @@ func classifyDatabaseError(errMsg string, jsonlCount int, jsonlAvailable bool) (
 		errorType = "Database is locked"
 		recoverySteps = "1. Check for running bd processes: ps aux | grep bd\n" +
 			"2. Kill any stale processes\n" +
-			"3. Remove stale locks: bd doctor --fix\n" +
-			"4. Retry: bd doctor"
+			"3. Run: bd doctor --fix (removes stale lock files including Dolt internal locks)\n" +
+			"4. If still stuck, manually remove: rm .beads/dolt-access.lock .beads/dolt/*/.dolt/noms/LOCK"
 
 	case strings.Contains(errMsg, "not a database") || strings.Contains(errMsg, "file is not a database"):
 		errorType = "File is not a valid SQLite database"
